neighborhoods.name: Downtown Port Huron
neighborhoods.description: Historic Blue Water Bridge border city with riverwalk, maritime heritage, and Thomas Edison boyhood home

housing.predominantType: Victorian houses, apartments, and older homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Port Huron. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Port Huron

neighborhoods.name: Fort Gratiot / Kimball
neighborhoods.description: Northern townships with shopping, lakefront parks, and more suburban character
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Port Huron's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Port Huron

neighborhoods.name: Marysville
neighborhoods.description: Small city on the St. Clair River with affordable housing and community character

housing.predominantType: Modest houses and affordable single-family homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Port Huron

neighborhoods.name: St. Clair / Marine City
neighborhoods.description: River communities south of Port Huron with boating culture, festivals, and family amenities

housing.predominantType: River houses, traditional homes, and waterfront properties

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Port Huron
